hero.heading: Taste the Soul of Italy
hero.subheading: Handcrafted pastas, wood-fired flavors, and la dolce vita — every table, every night.

scrollStory.heading: Our Heritage
scrollStory.body: Born in 2004 from a single recipe notebook passed down from Nonna Rosa, La Bella Vita has grown into a legacy of honest Italian cooking.

scrollStory.heading: The Kitchen
scrollStory.body: Every pasta is hand-rolled. Every sauce simmers for hours. Our wood-fired oven burns oak from local orchards.

scrollStory.heading: The Ingredients
scrollStory.body: San Marzano tomatoes from Campania. 00 flour from Molino Grassi. Parmigiano-Reggiano aged 36 months. Nothing less.

about.story: La Bella Vita began as a dream — a small corner restaurant where Italian-American chef Marco Ferretti could cook the way his grandmother taught him: slowly, lovingly, and without shortcuts. What started as 12 tables in a converted brownstone has grown into one of the city's most beloved dining destinations, yet our philosophy remains unchanged. We source the finest Italian imports, employ only classically trained chefs, and refuse to compromise on the quality that made us who we are.
about.sourcingNote: We partner directly with Italian importers and local organic farms. Our seafood is line-caught. Our meats are sourced from heritage breed farms. We believe great cooking starts with extraordinary ingredients — and that's non-negotiable.

chefs.name: Marco Ferretti
chefs.title: Executive Chef & Founder
chefs.bio: Born in Napoli and trained at Le Cordon Bleu Rome, Marco brings three decades of culinary mastery to every dish. His philosophy: the best meal is the one that reminds you of home.

chefs.name: Sofia Conti
chefs.title: Pastry Chef
chefs.bio: A master of Italian confectionery, Sofia trained at Iginio Massari's academy in Brescia. Her tiramisù has won awards; her cannoli have inspired pilgrimages.

chefs.name: Luca Bianchi
chefs.title: Head Sommelier
chefs.bio: With WSET Level 4 certification and a cellar of over 600 Italian labels, Luca curates wine pairings that transform every meal into a journey through the Italian peninsula.

timeline.event: La Bella Vita opens its doors in SoHo with 12 tables and Marco's grandmother's recipe book.
Recognised as 'Best New Trattoria' by NYC Eats magazine.
Second location opens in the West Village. Sofia Conti joins as Pastry Chef.
Awarded one Michelin star. First Italian wine cellar curated by Luca Bianchi.
Featured in The New York Times 'Top 50 Restaurants' list.
Launch of bespoke catering division and private events programme.

Over 600 Italian labels in our cellar
An evening at La Bella Vita

events.title: Quattro Mani — Chef's Table Dinner
events.description: An intimate 8-course tasting menu for 12 guests, prepared jointly by Chef Marco and a rotating guest chef from Italy. Paired wines selected by Luca.

events.title: Sunday Brunch Jazz
events.description: Every Sunday, a live jazz trio accompanies our extended brunch menu — fresh pastries, antipasti, eggs alla Fiorentina, Bellinis and Bloody Marias.

events.title: Masterclass: Fresh Pasta
events.description: Join Chef Marco for a 3-hour pasta-making masterclass. Learn to roll sfoglia, cut tagliatelle, and shape tortellini. Includes dinner and wine.

events.title: Vino & Formaggio Evening
events.description: An evening of Italian cheese education paired with 6 regional wines, guided by Luca Bianchi. Includes a curated cheese board and all accompaniments.

catering.intro: From intimate gatherings to grand celebrations, La Bella Vita's catering team brings the trattoria experience to your venue.

packages.name: Gran Tavola
packages.tagline: The full La Bella Vita experience
includes: Full service team with head sommelier

reviews.name: Samantha T.
reviews.text: Absolutely transcendent. The pappardelle al ragù transported me straight to Tuscany. Marco's kitchen is doing something truly special.

reviews.name: James R.
reviews.text: The Ossobuco was the best I've had outside of Milan. Luca's wine pairing recommendation was perfect. La Bella Vita is our anniversary restaurant now.

reviews.name: Elena M.
reviews.text: Sofia's tiramisù is life-changing. I've been to Italy many times and this rivals the best. The ambiance is warm and sophisticated. Perfetto!

reviews.name: David K.
reviews.text: Excellent food and impeccable service. The cacio e pepe was silky and perfectly seasoned. Only wish the reservations weren't so hard to get!

reviews.name: Priya N.
reviews.text: Hosted our team dinner here. The private dining room is stunning and the staff anticipated every need. The catering package was flawless.

reviews.name: Carlos V.
reviews.text: The pasta masterclass with Chef Marco was incredible! We made four types of pasta and the dinner afterward was exquisite. Highly recommended.

faq.question: Do you accept walk-ins?
faq.answer: We always keep a few tables available for walk-ins, but we strongly recommend reservations, especially on weekends. Walk-ins are welcome at the bar with the full menu available.

faq.question: Do you accommodate dietary restrictions?
faq.answer: Yes. Our menu is fully labelled with vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, and allergen information. Please inform your server of any dietary needs and we will be happy to accommodate.

faq.question: Is there a dress code?
faq.answer: Smart casual. We love guests who arrive looking and feeling great, but we don't enforce strict dress codes. Just leave the flip-flops at home.

faq.question: Do you host private events?
faq.answer: Yes! Our private dining room seats up to 16 guests. For larger events, please enquire about our full venue buyout and catering packages on the Events page.

faq.question: Is there parking available?
faq.answer: Our SoHo location offers valet parking Thursday–Sunday evenings. Street parking and nearby garages are available at all other times. The West Village location has street parking only.

faq.question: Can I bring my own wine?
faq.answer: We have a comprehensive Italian wine list curated by our sommelier. We do not offer corkage for outside bottles.

faq.question: Do you have a children's menu?
faq.answer: We don't have a dedicated children's menu, but our kitchen is happy to adapt many dishes for younger guests. Please ask your server.

faq.question: What is your cancellation policy?
faq.answer: We ask for 24 hours notice for cancellations. For parties of 6 or more, 48 hours notice is required. Late cancellations may be subject to a ₹25 per person fee.

careers.title: Sous Chef
careers.description: Join Chef Marco's kitchen as Sous Chef. You'll lead the pasta station, manage prep teams, and contribute to seasonal menu development.
requirements: 3+ years experience in a high-volume Italian kitchen
Deep knowledge of Italian cuisine and technique
Ability to work evenings, weekends, and holidays
benefits: Annual Italy trip for senior staff

careers.title: Front of House Manager
careers.description: We're looking for a polished, guest-obsessed FOH Manager to lead our West Village team. You'll own reservations, service standards, and team morale.
requirements: WSET Level 2 or above (or willingness to obtain)
Experience with OpenTable or similar reservation platforms

careers.title: Server — Part Time
careers.description: Passionate about Italian food and wine? We're looking for experienced servers to join our celebrated service team.
requirements: Knowledge of Italian cuisine and wine

careers.title: Pastry Commis
careers.description: Join Pastry Chef Sofia Conti's pastry kitchen. You'll assist in producing our dessert menu, pastries, and special occasion cakes.

contact.formNote: For reservations, please use our reservations page. This form is for general enquiries, private events, and media.

sections.title: Information We Collect
sections.body: We collect information you provide directly, such as your name, email, phone number, and reservation details when you make a booking or submit a contact form. We also collect usage data through analytics to improve our website experience.

sections.title: How We Use Your Information
sections.body: Your information is used solely to process reservations, respond to enquiries, and send relevant communications about events and updates (only with your consent). We never sell your data to third parties.

sections.title: Data Storage & Security
sections.body: All data is stored securely. Reservation data is retained for up to 24 months for operational purposes. You may request deletion at any time by contacting us.

sections.title: Cookies
sections.body: We use essential cookies to operate the website and optional analytics cookies. You can manage cookie preferences in your browser settings.

sections.title: Your Rights
sections.body: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. To exercise these rights, contact us at privacy@labellavita.com.

sections.title: Contact
sections.body: For privacy-related questions, contact our data team at privacy@labellavita.com or write to: La Bella Vita, 142 Greene Street, New York, NY 10012.

sections.title: Reservations
sections.body: Reservations are confirmed upon completion of the booking form. We require 24 hours notice for cancellations (48 hours for parties of 6 or more). Late cancellations or no-shows may be subject to a fee of ₹25 per guest.

sections.title: Dietary Restrictions
sections.body: While we take every precaution to accommodate dietary needs, our kitchen processes nuts, gluten, dairy, eggs, and shellfish. We cannot guarantee a completely allergen-free environment.

sections.title: Private Events
sections.body: Private event bookings require a signed agreement and deposit. All catering packages are subject to our standard terms and conditions provided upon booking.

sections.title: Gift Cards
sections.body: Gift cards are non-refundable and valid for 12 months from the date of purchase. They may be redeemed at any La Bella Vita location.

sections.title: Website Use
sections.body: The content on this website is for informational purposes only. Menus, prices, hours, and event details are subject to change without notice.

sections.title: Governing Law
sections.body: These terms are governed by the laws of the State of New York. Any disputes shall be resolved in the courts of New York County.
